\doxysubsection{Detailed Description}
Point electric dipole excitation. 

The dipole is defined by its complex polarization vector $\mathbf p$ and source position $\mathbf r_0$.

In a homogeneous background, fields are obtained from the dyadic free‑space Green\textquotesingle{}s functions. In a stratified background, reflected/transmitted contributions are added using tabulated layered‑media Green\textquotesingle{}s dyadics. 

Definition at line \mbox{\hyperlink{Dipole_8h_source_l00034}{34}} of file \mbox{\hyperlink{Dipole_8h_source}{Dipole.\+h}}.

\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}

\hline
\end{DoxyParams}

\begin{DoxyExceptions}{Exceptions}
{\em std\+::runtime\+\_\+error} & if layered Green\textquotesingle{}s function for field evaluation was not set. \\
\hline
\end{DoxyExceptions}
\begin{DoxyNote}{Note}
If the observation and source points are in the same layer, the homogeneous contribution is added automatically; layered contributions are added on top. 
\end{DoxyNote}
